user_message: Can you pull the context on Northwind Traders? I need to investigate the decline.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-07 10:25 EST
Subject: forecast prep - Action Required

There's been a change in our timeline for forecast prep. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
Date: 2024-02-21 11:29 EST
Subject: Re: forecast prep Discussion - Next Steps

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about forecast prep. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: Sandra Taylor <sandra.taylor@northwindtra.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-18 13:55 EST
Subject: Follow-up: forecast prep Review

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with forecast prep. What are the next steps from your side?

Meeting: forecast prep Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-03-14
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Priya (Northwind Traders)

[00:01:15] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is forecast prep.

[00:07:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is performance requirements.

[00:14:00] Priya (Northwind Traders): Our team lead reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): The timeline for budget approval is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:20:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): The timeline for performance requirements is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-A trap (Produces multi-page brief (loses utility)); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-A failure mode (Produces multi-page brief (loses utility))
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct forecast prep decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-A -- Produces multi-page brief (loses utility)
